title: Coaching & Consulting Prompt Studio
kicker: Guided prompt system for expert services
subtitle: Built for coaches, consultants, and advisors who need sharper offers, better client sessions, and recommendations people can act on. Pick a job, add the facts, and copy the finished prompt into the assistant you use.

profile.label: What you sell and who buys it
profile.placeholder: Example: a $3,000 operations advisory package for owner-led manufacturers with 20 to 100 employees
profile.helper: One or two sentences. Every prompt in this studio uses this.

profile.label: Your buyer in their own words
profile.placeholder: Example: 'We keep fixing the same fire every Friday' is how clients describe the problem
profile.helper: Use phrases real buyers or clients say.

profile.label: The offer or project you're working on now
profile.placeholder: Example: rebuilding the spring group program before enrollment opens

profile.label: Your main goal for the next 90 days
profile.placeholder: Example: enroll 12 right-fit clients without adding more sales calls

profile.label: Voice, proof, or constraints the AI should respect
profile.placeholder: Example: plainspoken, no income claims, two-person delivery team

categories.title: Positioning and Offers
categories.note: Turn experience into a clear reason to hire you.

categories.title: Sales Conversations
categories.note: Diagnose fit before the conversation turns into a pitch.

categories.title: Sessions and Delivery
categories.note: Make each client session produce a decision and a next move.

categories.title: Audits and Roadmaps
categories.note: Turn a messy business problem into ranked recommendations.

categories.title: Retention and Expansion
categories.note: Keep good clients moving without forcing an awkward upsell.

categories.title: Review and Improve
categories.note: Strengthen working material without losing the thinking already inside it.

workflows.title: Expertise Position
workflows.description: Identify the narrow expertise claim buyers can repeat and the evidence that supports it.
workflows.role: positioning strategist for expert services who must make the buyer, problem, and paid result unmistakable, with direct responsibility for the expertise position this prompt produces

fields.label: Describe the offer, expertise, or positioning you want to work on
fields.placeholder: Example: I help dental groups reduce front-desk turnover through a 10-week advisory engagement
fields.helper: Give the facts the output should use. Specifics beat background.

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required
fields.helper: Skip this if the field above already covers it.

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this expertise position.
Identify the narrow expertise claim buyers can repeat and the evidence that supports it.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished expertise position with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required
fields.helper: Skip this if the field above already covers it.

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this framework review.
Test whether a named method has real stages, decision rules, and a useful boundary.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished framework review with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

workflows.title: Advisory Material Review
workflows.description: Strengthen a worksheet, briefing, or training aid around the decision it must help someone make.
workflows.role: senior consulting editor who preserves sound thinking while fixing gaps, weak claims, and unclear next steps, with direct responsibility for the advisory material review this prompt produces

fields.label: Paste the material you want reviewed
fields.placeholder: Paste notes, a proposal, transcript, audit, or client message
fields.helper: The prompt keeps your core intent and works directly from this material.

fields.label: Numbers, evidence, or limits that should shape the answer
fields.placeholder: Example: two weeks, $4,000 budget, three client quotes, legal review required
fields.helper: Skip this if the field above already covers it.

task: Read my context and isolate the facts, constraints, and unknowns that matter to this advisory material review.
Strengthen a worksheet, briefing, or training aid around the decision it must help someone make.
Make the choices explicit. Rank options where a decision is needed, and tie every recommendation to something in my context.
Flag missing evidence and assumptions separately so I can review them before using the result.
rules: Don't pad the deliverable with a general introduction or a summary that repeats it.
When two reasonable choices compete, state the tradeoff and make one recommendation.
workflows.output: a finished advisory material review with the decision or artifact first, supporting reasoning, next actions, and a short assumptions check

advisors.title: The Positioning Partner
advisors.description: Finds the claim buyers can understand and competitors can't easily borrow.
advisors.role: a positioning consultant who tests every claim against buyer urgency, proof, and competitive sameness
advisors.focus: clarifying an expert service's strongest market position
questions: the costly problem clients already admit
the evidence behind the proposed claim
advisors.deliverable: a positioning decision, the rejected alternatives, and the proof needed to carry the claim

advisors.title: The Offer Architect
advisors.description: Turns scattered services into one engagement with a clean buying decision.
advisors.role: a service offer designer who balances buyer value, delivery reality, and scope control
advisors.focus: building or repairing a coaching or consulting offer
questions: who the engagement is and isn't for
what must change by the end
advisors.deliverable: a complete offer structure with scope, sequence, price logic, risks, and next test

advisors.title: The Call Coach
advisors.description: Diagnoses where a real sales conversation lost trust or momentum.
advisors.role: a consultative sales coach who listens for weak diagnosis, hidden objections, and rushed recommendations
questions: what the prospect said the problem costs
advisors.deliverable: a call diagnosis, better questions, and the exact next conversation to run

advisors.title: The Delivery Director
advisors.description: Keeps client work tied to decisions and visible movement.
advisors.role: a client delivery director who insists on ownership, evidence, and a useful meeting outcome
questions: the decision the client keeps avoiding
what has and hasn't been completed
advisors.deliverable: a delivery diagnosis and a short action plan with owners, dates, and review points

advisors.title: The Scope Guardian
advisors.description: Separates a useful favor from unpaid expansion that hurts the engagement.
advisors.role: a consulting operations lead who protects results, relationships, and delivery capacity
advisors.focus: handling scope, boundaries, and change requests
questions: what the client is asking for now
advisors.deliverable: the scope call, a client-ready response, and the paid or deferred options

advisors.title: The Renewal Strategist
advisors.description: Builds the renewal around evidence and the next business problem.
advisors.role: a client success strategist who earns renewals through adoption and delivered value
advisors.focus: planning renewals, referrals, and right-fit expansion
questions: what remains unresolved or newly possible
advisors.deliverable: a renewal path, evidence gaps, conversation plan, and fallback closeout
